title: betula occidentalis
intro: Betula occidentalis, also known as the paper birch, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, characterized by its white, papery bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Betula occidentalis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Betula occidentalis include tinctures, teas, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
